title: Coleus ater
taxonomy: Coleus ater is a species within the plant family Lamiaceae, which is also known as the mint family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its leaf shape and flower structure. The Coleus ater is native to the tropical regions of Africa and Asia. It is often used as an ornamental plant due to its vibrant colors and unique foliage. The plant's classification can also be further broken down into the genus Solenostemon, making the full classification Solenostemon scutellarioides, although it is sometimes referred to as Coleus ater.

intro: Coleus ater, also known as Coleus aromaticus or Coleus barbatus, is a perennial plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia, belonging to the Lamiaceae family. The health benefits of Coleus ater include its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which may aid in reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Coleus ater include flavonoids, phenolic acids, terpenoids, and essential oils, particularly sesquiterpenes and diterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Coleus ater is commonly used in traditional medicine and is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or decoction, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and skin conditions.
